content:
A “handoff seal” answers four questions:
What is this artifact in the naming grammar.
Which worlds, waves, and characters it touches.
Which prior artifacts it depends on.
What it expects the next AI or human to do.

The SEAL is embedded as a short header or footer, in plain text, and references its own LAYER-DOMAIN-TYPE-ID-VERSION name plus cross-links into $ARCHIVE_ROOT.

Example name:
SEAL-SYSTEM-SPEC-HANDOFF-0001-v1_0

────────────────────────
2. Self-Evolving OS Architecture and Core Roots

2.1 Root Directories and Roles

Define the following high-level roots as environment variables:

$OS_ROOT – the conceptual and file-system root of this creative OS.
$ARCHIVE_ROOT – the durable, append-only digital memory archive.
$PROMPT_ROOT – the canonical library of prompt templates and prompt shells.
$REPO_ROOT – the local or remote root hosting all git-managed repositories for $ORG_ID.
$ORG_ID – the neutral, non-branded organization identifier used in repository names.
